Nonetheless, it's a powerful feature that can enhance your development process, whether you're an experienced UI5 developer or just getting started. Let’s see what the future brings with more and more features getting introduced!

entry.title: 5 Major Generative AI Innovations Unveiled at SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
At SAP TechEd 2024, SAP revealed several key innovations centered around Generative AI, further enhancing its ecosystem and offering businesses powerful tools to drive digital transformation. These new capabilities focus on simplifying complex processes, boosting customization, and integrating AI into the core of enterprise applications. Here are the five major Generative AI announcements that will shape the future of SAP’s intelligent enterprise solutions.

1. Generative AI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud in SAP Build

One of the standout announcements is the introduction of the Generative AI toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud, embedded within SAP Build. This new toolkit allows users to execute data analysis and perform advanced machine learning tasks effortlessly.

By using the AI-powered capabilities available through Joule—SAP’s intelligent assistant—users can now complete complex tasks like generating time-series forecast models using natural language prompts. This eliminates the need for deep technical knowledge in machine learning, making AI accessible to business users and enabling faster, data-driven decision-making.

2. AI-Driven Optimization in SAP Integration Suite

SAP’s Integration Suite now comes equipped with Generative AI-driven optimization for custom scripts. This feature allows users to open the script editor and automatically receive AI-generated recommendations to optimize their code.

This enhancement empowers developers and business users alike by reducing manual effort and improving efficiency. The AI-driven optimizations make integration tasks smoother and more reliable, enhancing the overall performance of custom solutions.

3. Joule Studio: Design Custom AI Skills with Low-Code Tools

Joule Studio, a new feature within SAP Build, offers a low-code platform for creating custom AI skills that integrate seamlessly with your SAP landscape. With Joule Studio, developers can build intelligent applications without extensive coding knowledge, using a simplified interface.

For instance, if you need to bring ServiceNow tickets into Joule, you can easily create a custom skill using Joule Studio. The platform makes it simple to configure API parameters, deploy the skill, and instantly retrieve data from within Joule, making it an incredibly efficient solution for businesses looking to streamline their workflows and automate tasks.

Joule Studio will enter beta later this year, with general availability expected shortly after.

4. Enhanced Generative AI Hub: New Models and Capabilities

SAP also introduced a major upgrade to the Generative AI Hub, a platform that integrates state-of-the-art AI models with SAP applications. The hub now supports closed and open-source models from leading providers like Meta and Mistral, enabling businesses to harness the best of both foundational models and real-time data from SAP systems.

Key enhancements include:
- Orchestration services for managing AI workflows.
- Data masking and content filtering capabilities for improved privacy and security.
- New prompt templates and prompt management tools, making it easier to deploy AI models and create custom solutions.

Developers will also have access to new SDKs for Java, JavaScript, and ABAP, ensuring a seamless integration of AI into a variety of SAP environments.

5. SAP Knowledge Graph: Unlocking AI-Powered Insights

SAP’s Knowledge Graph, combined with the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine, is set to revolutionize how businesses interact with SAP data. The Knowledge Graph provides a vast repository of structured metadata, including over 450,000 ABAP tables, 80,000 CDS views, and more than 7 million properties and fields.

By linking Large Language Models (LLMs) with the semantic model of SAP, the Knowledge Graph enables powerful use cases, such as:
- AI-driven predictions and insights.
- Advanced information retrieval capabilities.
- Enhanced analytics across the SAP landscape.

This comprehensive dataset serves as a bridge between natural language processing (NLP) and SAP’s structured data, opening up new opportunities for AI-driven innovation. The SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine will be available in Q1 2025, offering developers a robust foundation for building advanced business applications.

Why These Announcements Matter

SAP’s focus on Generative AI at TechEd 2024 highlights a clear shift toward making AI more accessible to all users—whether they’re business professionals, developers, or data scientists. These innovations are designed to reduce the complexity of working with AI while enhancing its ability to solve real-world business problems.

With the introduction of tools like the Generative AI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud and Joule Studio, users can now build intelligent, AI-driven applications with ease. The Generative AI Hub and SAP Knowledge Graph further empower developers to leverage cutting-edge models and tap into SAP’s vast structured data for advanced analytics and business insights.

As these tools become available, businesses can expect a significant boost in their ability to automate processes, gain actionable insights, and create smarter, more efficient solutions in the SAP ecosystem.

Stay tuned for the full release of these features in the coming months, as SAP continues to lead the charge in bringing AI to enterprise systems in powerful and accessible ways.

entry.title: Latest Features of Goal management in 2H/2024 Release cycle
entry.content:
1. Enhancements to AI-Assisted Writing and Availability in More Areas - We can now improve the content of performance and development goals while creating it. It includes various goal plan fields like name, metric, description. But this will apply only to goal fields of text and textarea types.

Two new options are available now which are Translate and Analyze Text. The 'Translate' option would help in converting the given text into any of the 146 languages(that particular language enablement from the provisioning end is required). Also, the 'Analyze Text' option would exclude inclusiveness and other harmful content from the fields.

Limitation : The AI-assisted writing tool isn't yet supported in custom learning activities which may be linked to 'Development Goals'. See more

2. Link and Unalign Goals - Now, the users can link their own goals to other user's goal and vice-versa. From his/her own goal plan, a user can select Link Goal to link to other user's goal plan. Also, a user can navigate to other user's goal plan and select Link with Mine to link it to their own goal plan. The linkage would also show on the Goal Alignment Chart for a better visibility. 

Also, there is an option to remove the linkage using Unalign Goal option as well. See more

3. Roll-up Calculation Supported for Cascaded and Linked Goals - When the rollup-calc-type attribute is true, now you can calculate sum or avg for the cascaded and linked goals in the bizx-actual, bizx-target and bizx-pos fields. Whenever any goal is cascaded or linked, it will generate two records in the audit trail. One would be 'Cascaded' or 'Linked', and the record would be 'Modified'.

As this link would be shown on the Goal Alignment Chart, it will enhance goal tracking of the linked and cascaded goals. See more

4. Hide "Create from Scratch" Option for Goal Creation - Previously, there were three fixed options to create goals on the goals page. Those are Create from Scratch, Copy from Goal Plan, Copy from Library. Now there is an option to hide the Create from Scratch option from the goals page. For this, the turnoff-add-personal-goal-button switch should be set to on in the goal plan template. See more

5. Multi-Line Display for Milestone Fields - In the milestone section of the goal details view, multi-line display is now supported for fields whose type is set to textarea, such as desc and actual. See more

6. Goal Modification Email Jobs Available in Admin Center - Earlier, we had many jobs not available in the admin center and we could only create them from provisioning end. A major 'Goal Modification Email' is now available in the admin center. In the job parameters, a new value, Past Week, is now available in the Job Parameters. See more

7. Latest Goal Management Allows Duplicate Assignment of Team Goals During Import - The Allow Duplicate Assignment of Team Goals feature is now available in the latest Goal Management. This feature is available only during goal import. See more

8. Copy Goals on Behalf of Others - In the latest goal management, managers have the option to navigate to his/her direct report's goal plan and copy the template from there. The goals created would then be visible in the user's goal plan. This is done so that the managers have the option to create a goal for their direct report on behalf of the employee. See more

9. User Experience Enhancements to the Latest Goal Management - These are as follows:

  1. Weights are now displayed for team goals on goal cards.
  2. Users can now include comments in the PDF output of goals.
  3. Users can now include inactive users in search results in People Selector.
  4. On the goal details page, we made the left-hand sections collapsible. Users can now choose the Show more button to expand the full content of milestone fields configured as the textarea type.
  5. On the goal details page, we moved the entry to audit history to a new section on the right hand.
  6. On the goal details page, we made all milestone fields visible without the need to choose the Manage Milestones button. The milestone table is now horizontally scrollable.
  7. Users can now choose the Show more button to expand the full content of milestone fields configured as the textarea type.
  8. In the Goal Alignment dialog, the column headers are updated to be more inclusive.
  9. Users can now search for assignees on the team goal details page.
  10. On the landing page, the tab names for performance and development goals are updated to use the plural form.
  11. More goals can now be displayed in goal list views.
  12. A new column Co-owner Username is now supported in both Story reports and Table reports for master team goals.
  13. In audit history, "Shared" and "Unshared" are now supported as new action types for team goals.  See more

10. Enhancements to AI-Assisted Goal Creation - Several enhancements to AI-assisted goal creation have been made in the latest Goal Management. See more

Thanks for reading. Let me know your insights in the comments below. 🙂

entry.title: Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Imagine if you could converse with your own business data, leveraging the human-like text responses of a Large Language Model (LLM), and have access to context-aware information to make well-informed decisions. We're excited to introduce a central capability at SAP that grounds LLM responses in business data and provides users with relevant, reliable, and responsible output tailored to their specific needs. By feeding relevant and up-to-date information to an LLM, we reduce model hallucinations - all without the need to finetune the model. 

SAP leverages retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to deliver a central and domain-agnostic service that is easy to use and scales across all business functions. This service also fact-checks LLM-generated statements by tracing information back to its sources, enabling users to act with confidence based on verified data. 

In this blog, let’s dive into two capabilities:  

  • Document grounding, a RAG feature for Joule, SAP’s AI copilot 
  • The Grounding capability in the orchestration module of Generative AI hub to build custom AI applications 

Document grounding: Enhancing the Q&A feature of Joule  

The Document grounding capability allows customers to use their own unstructured or semi-structured documents to leverage the Q&A feature of Joule. By connecting their own repositories, such as SharePoint, customers can gain fast and reliable insights from their business documents, saving time spent on finding, opening, and reading documents. 

Real-world use-case: SAP SuccessFactors is a perfect example of how Document grounding can revolutionize employee self-service capabilities via Joule. Let's say an employee needs to find information on their company's parental leave policy. Instead of sifting through lengthy documents, the employee simply asks Joule: "What is the maximum entitlement for parental leave?" Joule, powered by the Document grounding service, retrieves the relevant information from their company's HR policy documents (previously indexed) and provides them with a concise, accurate answer.

View of the Q&A feature in Joule, enhanced with Document Grounding 

 

Business Impact: This capability offers an intuitive user experience, improves self-service efficiency, and reduces the total cost of ownership (TCO) with 24x7 HR support. It's not just an AI feature; it's a whole new way of empowering teams with knowledge at their fingertips. It can lead to potential efficiency improvements of 70% reduction in employee time needed to investigate HR policies & 81% reduction in time spent by HR on ticket handling, showcasing the tangible impact of this capability. 

But the benefits extend beyond just HR. Imagine a sales executive quickly accessing the latest product specifications, or a finance leader getting real-time insights into company financial policies. By making information more accessible and actionable, Document grounding has the potential to transform decision-making processes across the entire organization.  

What’s under the hood? 

SAP’s Document grounding service leverages RAG to add vital context to LLM responses through a multi-step indexing & retrieval process: 

  1. Indexing Pipeline: This component ingests data from diverse sources, processes it, and splits it into manageable chunks. It then generates embeddings of this data and stores them in the SAP HANA Vector Database. 
  2. Vector Database: The SAP HANA Vector Engine queries these vector embeddings. 
  3. Retrieval: For every user query, vector embeddings are generated using the LLM model and a search in the HANA Vector Database finds relevant text chunks. 

Find more technical and commercial information about Document Grounding in our dedicated Help Portal page. 

A Grounding capability via generative AI hub for your customized AI applications 

While Document grounding is meant to cover Joule scenarios, SAP is also addressing the needs of developer persona who would want to benefit from the RAG capability within their customized AI applications. 

By the end of 2024, the document grounding capability will be fully integrated in the orchestration module of the generative AI hub, alongside capabilities like prompt templating, data masking, and content filter. The mechanism exposed sooner in this article will be applicable via the consumption of the Orchestration service or direct API consumption (indexing and retrieval APIs).  

 Architecture of the Grounding capability inside Generative AI hub

What’s coming next? 

Among many other features, our roadmap through 2025 includesmulti-language support, contextualization, extended list of supported document types and formats as well as documents repositories.  

Also, to enhance the quality of indexing and retrieval, SAP is working on backstage technical layers like Automatic RAG (choosing the best chunking strategy automatically), advanced retrieval techniques, Knowledge Graph integration which comes preloaded with a comprehensive representation of SAP’s business entities (including ABAP tables, CDS views, APIs, and key data models), and will play a critical role in grounding AI models and enabling them to deliver more reliable, context-aware insights.  

Any questions? Feel free to contact Grounding product management.  

Email: sebastien.beghelli@sap.com 

Interested in learning more? Refer to the links below 

  • TechEd 2024 session about Document Grounding and the Grounding capability inside GenAI Hub
  • [SAP BTP Onboarding Series]Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - set up guide
  • Document grounding Discovery center page

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 Announcements - Consolidated and Simplified
entry.content:
In this blog, I have consolidated everything related to SAP TechEd 2024 in one place! Hope this will help the community.

Note: Don't miss the list of all the links of news, recordings, source-code, demo etc. towards the end of this blog.

 

First let's have a quick summary of all the key announcements.

 

 

Let's go in detail.

SAP Build + ABAP Cloud

  • SAP Build unifies the entire development and extension capabilities and will now also include ABAP Cloud.
  • Technically, it means that SAP Build will integrate ABAP development tools and ABAP development environments for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP BTP.
  • Developers will also be able to create and monitor their ABAP Cloud projects directly from SAP Build.
  • It will be available by Q4 2024.
  • SAP Build Lobby will be the central starting point for all extension projects.

Demo of SAP Build Lobby

 

SAP Build for S/4HANA Extension Development

  • A new extensibility wizard is available now which will let developers access SAP Build directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
  • With this, developers can extend custom fields, business logic and processes either on-stack (deployed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP environment) or side-by-side (deployed in SAP BTP).
  • In Q4 2024, the extensibility wizard will let developers create custom SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 applications, leveraging the full set of features available in SAP Build.

 

New Generative AI Capabilities of SAP Build with Joule

  • New capabilities in SAP Build Code such as code explanation and documentation search will reduce development time for Java and JavaScript developers (Q4 2024).
  • Developers will also be able to automate workflows in SAP Build Process Automation with assistance from generative AI (Q4 2024)
  • ABAP developers will be able to generate high-quality code and unit tests with Joule that comply with SAP’s ABAP Cloud Development model, and also generate explanations for legacy code, making it easier to modernize legacy codebases and migrate to a clean core (Q1 2025)
  • Developers will also be able to enhance Joule with custom skills using Joule studio in SAP Build, integrating it seamlessly with both SAP and non-SAP applications (Q1 2025)

For more information on SAP Build, refer to SAP Build.

 

Joule with SAP Build Work Zone

  • Joule will also be integrated into SAP Build Work Zone (Q4 2024)
  • It will be available in SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, the SAP Start site and the SAP Mobile Start app.

 

Joule with Collaborative AI Agents

  • SAP will infuse Joule with multiple autonomous AI agents that will combine their unique expertise to collaboratively accomplish complex workflows (Q4 2024)
  • With an in-built agentic capability, Joule can now solve complex business problems for you through planning, self-reflection, logical reasoning and collaboration.
  • Developers will be able to extend Joule by building their own autonomous AI agent use cases with SAP Build (Q1 2025)

Joule Agent Demo

 

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Joule will be available for all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers (Q4 2024)
  • Joule capabilities will expand to support key functions including sales professionals, warehouse clerks and project managers (Q1 of 2025)

 

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Following the introduction of Joule into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in July, SAP launched a feature pack with new use cases that let business users instruct Joule with simple commands.
  • The feature pack includes a new implementation wizard that installs a model outlining criteria to evaluate extensions built with low-code, no-code and pro-code tools on SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP.
  • Joule will be incorporated into the top 300 most-frequently-used SAP Fiori apps (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will also be able to take master data change requests within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, so users don’t have to navigate across multiple menus.

 

Joule in SAP BTP

  • Joule will be available to use with SAP HANA Cloud in SAP BTP cockpit (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be incorporated into SAP BTP cockpit (Q4 2024)
  • Joule can be directed to perform transactional scenarios like creating a subaccount, onboarding users, and answering questions about SAP BTP administrations in general. 

 

Joule in Other SAP Solutions

  • Joule will be available in the SAP Service Cloud solution and SAP Concur solutions (Q1 2025)
  • SAP now offers Joule across the SAP Integrated Business Planning solution, the SAP Digital Manufacturing solution, and the SAP Asset Performance Management application.
  • SAP will continue to roll out Joule across the entire SAP Supply Chain portfolio.
  • Joule will be available in the SAP Sales Cloud solution with navigational and task-management assistance (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will help SAP LeanIX solutions users manage and optimize their IT landscape (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be available with SAP Signavio (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be available in SAP Mobile Start (Q4 2024)      

 

Joule studio in SAP Build

  • SAP will release Joule studio in SAP Build (Q4 2024).
  • Joule studio will provide a dedicated environment for businesses to create, deploy, monitor and manage custom skills for Joule developed with low-code, no-code capabilities.
  • Custom skills, which complement Joule’s out-of-the-box capabilities from SAP, extend conversational AI to organization-specific workflows.
  • Additionally, customers can now integrate any third-party system into Joule for an entirely integrated conversational user experience.

 

SAP Knowledge Graph

  • SAP announces the addition of a knowledge graph engine to SAP HANA Cloud (Q1 2025)
  • The knowledge graph engine will let users identify and understand complex relationships in their data not otherwise discoverable using traditional data modeling tools.
  • SAP announces SAP Knowledge Graph, a new solution that will unlock the full value of SAP data by connecting it with the rich business context captured in SAP applications.
  • Accessible through SAP Datasphere and Joule in Q1 2025, it will help root AI in SAP-specific business semantics, reducing the risk of inaccurate or irrelevant results.
  • In its initial release, this solution will focus on SAP S/4HANA, providing a semantic layer that connects over 450,000 ABAP tables, 80,000 CDS views and thousands of SAP Fiori applications, along with OData APIs and entity sets.

 

SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine

  • Knowledge graph engine will be added to SAP HANA Cloud (Q1 2025)
  • By combining the vector engine and the knowledge graph engine, you can harness the power of structured and unstructured data together.

 

SAP Expands Strategic AI Partnerships

  • Anthropic: SAP introduces Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet in Amazon Bedrock to generative AI hub.
  • HCLTech: HCLTech announces a newly built AI Center of Excellence in Munich focused on developing AI use cases for the AI Foundation on SAP BTP.
  • IBM: SAP announces the addition of IBM Granite, IBM’s flagship series of transparent and efficient LLMs, to the growing repository of AI models available in generative AI hub.
  • Meta: SAP continues to execute its multi-vendor strategy for LLMs with the addition of Meta Llama 3.1 70B, which is built to deliver performance and cost efficiency.
  • Mistral AI: SAP continues to expand its partnership with Mistral AI by adding Mistral Large 2 to generative AI hub.
  • PwC: PwC announces it is using generative AI capabilities based on SAP’s generative AI hub.

 

Generative AI Hub + ABAP

  • Developers will be able to access generative AI hub capabilities from custom ABAP applications using the ABAP AI SDK powered by the intelligent scenario lifecycle management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud functionality. 
  • Using the ABAP AI SDK, ABAP developers can easily infuse AI capabilities into their custom applications and extensions.

 

Generative AI Hub – New LLMs Support

New LLMs will be available for generative AI hub by the end of 2024, including:

  • Aleph Alpha Pharia-1
  • Amazon Titan Image Generator
  • IBM Granite
  • Mistral Large 2
  • OpenAI Dall-E 3

 

Generative AI Hub – New Data Centers

New data centers for generative AI hub by the end of Q4 2024:

  • AWS: Singapore
  • Google Cloud Platform: Australia (Sydney); Japan (Osaka)
  • Microsoft Azure: Australia (Sydney); Switzerland (Zurich)

 

Generative AI Hub – Other Updates

  • We will be able to integrate advanced AI capabilities into web apps with new SDK support for ABAP, Java, and JavaScript in generative AI hub (Q4 2024)
  • Orchestration improvements to the generative AI hub capability in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad that will enable document grounding and indexing pipelines (Q4 2024)

 

New generative AI functionality for customers move to the cloud faster

  • SAP announced a new generative AI functionality that helps customers move to the cloud faster (Q1 2025).
  • Accessed through the SAP Cloud ALM solution, customers and consultants will be able to get expert answers, proactive guidance, and task automation aligned with the RISE with SAP Methodology.

 

SAP Expands its Portfolio of Learning and Certification Offerings

  • SAP launches new role-based certifications, free training materials and hands-on opportunities for developers and tech professionals.
  • These includes 60 new self-paced learning journeys, trial experiences and solution demonstrations that help tech professionals benefit from the latest fully integrated and practical training.

 

SAP Datasphere

  • SAP announced new embedded data lake capabilities for SAP Datasphere (Q4 2024)
  • Built on SAP HANA Cloud SQL on files and data lake file service capabilities, it will expand the business data fabric architecture with a data lake option, complementing existing storage solutions.
  • This helps businesses access, manage, and analyze data across cloud and hybrid environments while preserving its context and logic, improving decision-making, operational efficiency, and AI-driven innovation.

 

New embedded data lake capabilities

  • An integrated object store, which provides a simpler way to store large amounts of data, making it easier for businesses to expand their storage as needed.
  • Spark compute, which facilitates efficient data transformation and processing based on existing SAP Datasphere data integration capabilities.
  • SQL on files functionality, which lets developers access data in the integrated object store without needing to physically copy the data.

These capabilities let users import and integrate data at scale while maintaining a consistent user experience that ensures seamless data integration from both SAP sources (including SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Warehouse) and non-SAP sources while preserving the original structure and context of the data.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

  • SAP announced general availability of the new SAP Analytics Cloud compass feature (Q1 2025)
  • SAP Analytics Cloud compass feature can be used to model complex risk scenarios and simulate a broad range of probable outcomes.

 

SAP Integration Suite

  • SAP Integration Suite, starter edition will offer an unlimited number of prepackaged SAP integrations and a fixed number of customizable integrations for GROW with SAP and SAP SuccessFactors customers (Q4 2024)
  • New integration adapters will allow users manage data from various third-party technology platforms, including Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more.
  • AI supported custom script optimization (2025)
  • AI support iFlow creation is now Generally Available

entry.title: IBM Granite.13b.chat Large Language Model- Now GA on the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
SAP’s generative AI Hub is the central place for BTP application developers to access and use generative AI tools and models, and developers can now choose IBM Granite as their large language model (LLM). This article will explain some of Granite’s unique characteristics and then show you how to access and use it.

IBM Granite.13b.chat is a decoder, English-only, enterprise-grade LLM and part of a series of IBM foundation models for generative artificial intelligence tasks. IBM stands behind Granite LLMs with IP indemnification protection according to its Standard Terms and Conditions, to provide users with peace of mind. Granite.13b.chat was trained on enterprise-relevant content that meets rigorous data governance, regulatory, and risk criteria defined and enforced by the IBM AI Ethics Code and Chief Privacy Office. You can read the details here: https://www.ibm.com/downloads/cas/X9W4O6BM. Granite.13b.chat delivers high accuracy for retrieval augmented generation (RAG), summarization, content generation, named entity recognition, insight extraction, and classification.

A considerable portion of Granite training data is from business domains. For business-specific tasks in domains such as finance, some IBM performance benchmarks suggest that the accuracy of Granite models is on par with larger models (70B parameter+) at lower latency and, due to its smaller size (13B parameter), lower infrastructure spending.

AI capabilities in SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad that utilize the generative AI hub can leverage Granite.13b.chat.

Get started by:

  1. Learning how to use foundational models on SAP AI Core (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-consumption-llm.html), and
  2. learning how to prompt Granite.13b.chat (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/docs/content/wsj/analyze-data/fm-models-ibm-chat.html?context=wx&audience=wdp).

For more information about Granite.13b.chat and instruction-tuning details, see the model card (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/login?context=wx&sign_in_target=%2Fwx%2Fsamples%2Fmodels%2Fibm%2Fgranite-13b-chat-v2%3Fcontext%3Dwx%3Fcontext%3Dwx%26audience%3Dwdp).

entry.title: SAP BTP: The Foundation for Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The digital revolution is in full swing, and at its core lies the transformative power of artificial intelligence (AI). For the tech-savvy enterprise, AI is not just a buzzword, but a strategic imperative. SAP Business AI emerges as a key enabler of this transformation, offering a robust suite of tools that empower businesses to optimize operations, automate processes, and gain a competitive edge.

SAP: A Legacy of Innovation in the Enterprise Landscape

Since its inception in 1972, SAP has been a pioneer in enterprise technology, guiding businesses through technological shifts and helping them leverage new opportunities. With SAP Business AI, SAP continue this tradition, delivering a cutting-edge platform that harnesses the power of AI to redefine business processes.

SAP Business AI: More Than Just Automation

SAP Business AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses leverage data and technology. It's not just about automating tasks; it's about fundamentally changing how businesses operate and innovate. Think of it as an intelligent layer embedded within your existing SAP landscape, constantly learning and adapting to optimize processes and drive better outcomes.

Key Components and Applications: A Technical Perspective

SAP Business AI is deeply integrated across SAP's entire product portfolio, providing seamless access to AI capabilities within the context of everyday business processes. Here's a closer look at the technical components that make it so powerful:

Across key areas like Finance, Supply Chain, HR, Procurement, Marketing & Commerce, Sales & Services, and IT. This broad reach allows businesses to leverage AI in every aspect of their operations.

SAP Business AI provides a dual approach to AI, offering both pre-built AI services and the tools to build custom AI solutions. For specific business processes, SAP offers ready-to-use AI services that can be easily adapted to unique workflows. These services cover areas such as document processing, data attribute recommendations, and Robotic Process Automation.

At the heart of SAP Business AI lies a robust data foundation. This foundation includes SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) as the underlying infrastructure, along with services like SAP Datasphere, Analytics Cloud, and Integration Suite. These tools make it easy to connect, model, and analyze data across different applications, ensuring that AI algorithms have access to the high-quality data they need.

SAP also recognizes the importance of collaboration in the AI ecosystem. Establishing strategic partnerships with other industry leaders, such as Microsoft, Google, AWS, and IBM, to leverage their expertise and technologies. This collaborative approach ensures that SAP Business AI remains at the forefront of AI innovation, so that with leading AI vendors and large language model (LLM) providers, giving customers access to the latest innovations and ensuring flexibility in their AI deployments.

SAP Business AI Approach: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible

SAP's approach to AI is guided by three core principles, as highlighted in the image: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible.

Relevant: SAP Business AI is designed to be relevant from day one. Its deep integration with SAP applications ensures that AI capabilities are readily available and easy to use within the context of everyday business processes.

Reliable: SAP has a long-standing reputation for delivering reliable enterprise software, and this commitment extends to SAP Business AI. The platform is built on a foundation of high-quality data, rigorous testing, and robust security measures, ensuring that businesses can trust its accuracy and performance.

Responsible: SAP recognizes the ethical implications of AI and is committed to developing and deploying AI in a responsible manner. This includes addressing concerns around bias, discrimination, and privacy, and ensuring that AI is used to augment human capabilities, not replace them.

Assessing Business AI Infused Across the SAP Product Portfolio

AI is already deeply ingrained in SAP's enterprise applications and business technology, used by tens of thousands of their cloud customers. AI-powered capabilities, based on proprietary deep learning and machine learning algorithms, are driving significant business value across SAP's cloud solutions. These capabilities include intelligent document processing, personalized recommendations, natural language processing, forecasting, and more. This means that businesses can leverage AI capabilities within their existing SAP solutions, without having to implement separate AI tools or platforms.

Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

Transforming Business Applications with Generative AI

Generative AI is ushering in a new era of possibilities for business software. It enables the creation of new content, summarization of complex information, automated code writing, and much more. This new generation of AI can reason through business problems and offer solutions that were previously unimaginable.
SAP customers can expect to see tangible value from generative AI use cases across the entire SAP portfolio: Generative AI at SAP 

 Let's explore how generative AI is transforming key areas within the SAP ecosystem:

  • Cloud ERP: Streamlining processes like the verification of delivery notes, automating document processing with greater accuracy and efficiency. For example, imagine automatically processing thousands of invoices with minimal human intervention, using AI to extract relevant data and match it with purchase orders.
  • Human Capital Management: Freeing up managers' time by automating tasks like job postings, candidate sourcing, and performance reviews, while also reducing bias in these processes. AI can help identify the best candidates for a job by analyzing resumes and social media profiles, personalize onboarding experiences by providing tailored content and recommendations, and even predict employee attrition by identifying patterns in employee data.
  • Spend Management & Business Network: Empowering category managers with the information they need to make strategic procurement decisions, leveraging market dynamics and cost structure insights. AI can analyze spending patterns, identify cost-saving opportunities, and optimize supplier relationships by analyzing market data and predicting price fluctuations.
  • Customer Relationship Management: Elevating customer engagement by providing sales teams with personalized insights into customer preferences and purchase history, leading to tailored interactions and improved customer satisfaction. Imagine AI-powered chatbots that provide instant support and personalized recommendations to customers, improving customer satisfaction and reducing support costs. AI can also help sales teams prioritize leads, personalize marketing campaigns, and identify upselling and cross-selling opportunities.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Simplifying application development by enabling AI-powered code generation and test script creation, allowing developers to focus on more complex tasks. This can significantly reduce development time and costs, allowing businesses to innovate faster. Tools like SAP Build Code help automate code generation and testing, reducing development time and costs. For example, a developer could describe a desired functionality in natural language, and SAP Build Code would generate the corresponding code automatically.

Recognizing the Integration of SAP BTP with Business AI

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) serves as the underlying platform for SAP Business AI, providing the infrastructure and tools needed to build and deploy AI-powered applications.

Here are some build-in components of SAP BTP that are particularly relevant for AI development:

  • SAP Build Code: This tool empowers developers to build applications faster with the help of generative AI. The Joule copilot provides intelligent code suggestions and automates repetitive coding tasks, increasing developer productivity. For example, Joule can automatically generate code for common tasks, such as creating user interfaces or connecting to databases.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: This no-code solution enables business users to automate workflows and processes without writing any code. Its intuitive drag-and-drop interface and AI capabilities make it easy to create and deploy automations. This empowers business users to automate tasks without relying on IT, increasing agility and efficiency.
  • SAP HANA Cloud: This powerful in-memory database is optimized for handling large volumes of data and complex analytical queries, making it ideal for AI applications. Its ability to process data in memory allows for real-time analysis and insights, which is crucial for many AI use cases.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud: This cloud-based analytics platform provides a comprehensive suite of tools for data visualization, analysis, and planning. The integration of Joule, SAP's generative AI copilot, further enhances the platform's capabilities, enabling users to gain deeper insights from their data. For example, users can ask Joule to "show me the top 10 customers by revenue" or "predict next quarter's sales".

Focusing on the future of AI in the enterprise

SAP Business AI is more than just a technology; it's a strategic enabler for the modern enterprise. By seamlessly integrating AI across its product portfolio, SAP is empowering businesses to optimize processes, automate tasks, gain valuable insights, and ultimately achieve better outcomes. With a strong commitment to relevance, reliability, and responsibility, SAP Business AI is paving the way for a future where businesses can harness the full potential of AI in an ethical and sustainable manner.

Ready to delve deeper into the world of SAP Business AI? Here are some resources to get you started:

  • Artificial­ Intelligence­ |­ SAP ­Business ­AI
  • AI Solutions on SAP Business Technology Platform
  • AI Ethics | SAP Global AI Ethics Policy
  • SAP ­Community:­ Artificial­ Intelligence­ and­ Machine­ Learning­ at­ SAP
  • SAP ­Learning:­ Tutorials­ for­ SAP ­Developers­
  • Discover SAP Business AI features
  • Implement your use cases on SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Learning: how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: 2H 2024 Release Highlights for the Compensation Module
entry.content:
The 2nd Half 2024 Release of SAP SuccessFactors rolled out on Preview environments on October 14 (Production: November 15-17) and is bringing tons of great new features for the Compensation module, please find my top 5 below with demo recordings as well as the list of all new features here !

 

 

✅Executive Review Access Based on Route Step

Huge time-saver for Compensation administrators that now have full independence from security admins (RBP) when running the compensation cycle.

Please find a 23' demo with insights here: https://youtu.be/f7OhtfacS-k 

 

 

✅AI-assisted writing for Compensation

AI is here for the Compensation module to help write quality texts quickly to justify increases or spot awards ! very exciting.

Please find a 3' demo here: https://youtu.be/6yM0d7g_sxw 

AI-assisted Writing is available through our Embedded AI offering across many areas of the SuccessFactors suite. Please note: AI-assisted Writing is not available through Joule.

Additional details:
✅ AI-assisted Writing is a Premium AI Feature within SuccessFactors Business AI.
✅ Thus, AI-assisted Writing requires SAP AI Units per use.
✅ That includes the actions available such as Enhance Writing, Change Tone, and more.

More information can be found here.

 

✅Editable Statements Transport

Manually re-creating compensation statements in a target environment as part of configuration migration was one of the few remaining time-consuming items for implementation partners. It is now solved with this new enhancement that allows us to export standard statements as XML and import them into a target environment while keeping them as editable in the Statement Designer UI. With the support for multiple languages as well as the rich text editor added in 1H 2024 standard statements are now the best they've ever been. 

Please find a 5' demo with insights here: https://youtu.be/7eGOgC5fo1Q 

 

If you are going to 2024 SuccessConnect in Lisbon, don't miss my colleague Nestor's session where he will show an example of a great-looking statement he was able to build leveraging this 1H 2024 enhancement:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 


✅API for Compensation Data (read)

Allows us to feed planning data to other systems in real time (I got the requirement in my most recent implementation). 

Enhancement official page.

 



✅Publish to MDF for Total Comp templates

What I considered the latest major gap of Total Comp has been addressed and we can now build end-to-end complex workarounds for specific requirements.

Enhancement official page. 

For a demo of publish to MDF (first brought to Compensation templates in 2H 2021) please see this blog.

 

 

 

 

All the best,

Xavier

 

(If you found this blog and its recordings useful please consider giving it a Kudo)

entry.title: Rethinking AI’s Role in SAP: End of Bureaucracy = a Path to True Agility and Innovation
entry.content:
AI only for SAP Consultants?

In the world of SAP IT, many believe that AI will make developers and functional teams more productive by automating tasks and helping with decision-making. This, in theory, should make work faster and everyone happier. However, there's a major concern—many consultants and workers are worried that AI will take away their jobs. This fear causes resistance to AI and slows down further automation efforts.

But focusing only on how AI can help developers and functional teams might be too narrow. AI could be even more helpful if it were used to simplify the bureaucratic processes that slow down innovation in large organizations. Over the years, many SAP projects have tried to use agile methods, which focus on working quickly and adapting to changes. Unfortunately, they often fail because of outdated processes and too much bureaucracy, which make it hard to truly embrace agile.

 

So, instead of thinking about AI as just a tool to improve developer productivity, why not consider how it could reduce bureaucracy and make SAP projects more agile? In large organizations with thousands of employees, complex approval processes and heavy paperwork slow down progress. This makes it hard to move quickly or innovate.

AI to enable SAP innovation by reducing management operations 

AI could be used to simplify and automate project management tasks like resource allocation, project timelines, and risk management. It could help streamline decision-making and approval processes, allowing project managers to focus on bigger-picture strategy instead of getting bogged down in administrative work. If AI can take over these time-consuming tasks, project teams could collaborate more effectively and work faster.

In a large SAP environment, AI could remove the red tape that holds back agile workflows. This would allow companies to move towards true innovation, without the fear of job losses. In fact, AI could create new opportunities for employees to focus on strategic work, rather than repetitive tasks, thus increasing their value in the organization.

Conclusion - start with eliminating the bureaucratic barriers

In conclusion, AI’s true potential in SAP isn’t just about making developers and functional teams more productive. It’s about breaking down the bureaucratic barriers that prevent organizations from becoming agile and innovative. By applying AI to these outdated processes, companies can speed up project delivery, foster creativity, and unlock the full benefits of agile methodologies.

Want more?

Follow https://www.linkedin.com/in/michalkrawczyk/ on linked for a new episode of my webcast where I will discuss this topic in detail.

entry.title: Integrating SAP CX AI Toolkit with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Abstract

The SAP CX AI Toolkit is an advanced suite of AI-powered solutions designed to enhance the entire portfolio of SAP CX (customer experience) solutions (e.g. sales, services and commerce) by leveraging cutting-edge proprietary AI models and large language models. Especially for SAP Commerce Cloud, it offers AI-based features that allow you to e.g.:

  • extract and enrich product tags with image and text-based attributes, ensuring improved search accuracy and more personalized product recommendations,
  • generate compelling product content, ranging from captivating descriptions to engaging images,
  • enhances product discoverability through innovative visual search capabilities, making it easier for customers to find exactly what they need.

The video Using SAP CX AI Toolkit in SAP Commerce Cloud provides a brief overview of major features along with practical demonstrations. But for a full list of features supported by the SAP CX AI Toolkit, please visit the dedicated SAP CX AI Toolkit Features page and the SAP CX AI Toolkit Help Portal.

In this blog post, I will guide you through the major steps required to integrate the SAP CX AI Toolkit with SAP Commerce Cloud.

Prerequisite

You should have:

  • a provisioned SAP CX AI toolkit tenant with admin role access.
  • an SAP Commerce Cloud instance with admin role access.

Overview of the Major Integration Steps

  1. Prepare the SAP Commerce Cloud server
  2. Set up the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant
  3. Validate the connection and synchronize the data

Let's dive into each of these steps in more detail.

Step 1: Prepare the SAP Commerce Cloud Server

1.1 Prepare the manifest.json file and build/deploy SAP Commerce Cloud

To prepare the SAP Commerce Cloud server for integration with the SAP CX AI Toolkit, you need to ensure the necessary extensions are included in the 'manifest.json' file:

 

inboundservices
integrationbackoffice
integrationbackofficetest
integrationmonitoringbackoffice
integrationservices
odata2services
odata2webservices
outboundservices
outboundsync
outboundsyncbackoffice
webhookbackoffice
webhookservices

 

Next, make sure the 'odata2webservices' service is configured in one of the nodes in the 'manifest.json' file:

 

    "aspects": [
        {
            "name": "backoffice",
            "properties": [
                ...
            ],
            "webapps": [
                ...,
                {
                    "name": "odata2webservices"
                },

 

The odata2webservices facilitate communication between SAP Commerce Cloud and the SAP CX AI Toolkit. Please ensure you know which server node will host the 'odata2webservices' service. (In my case, the backoffice node will host it for demo purposes, but typically the API node is more suitable.)

Finally, trigger a build based on the prepared 'manifest.json' file and deploy the successful build to a specific SAP Commerce Cloud environment.

1.2 Check/validate the settings of the necessary integration objects

On the successfully deployed SAP Commerce Cloud, we need to ensure the following predefined integration objects are correctly configured:

  • IntegrationService
  • ScriptService
  • OutboundChannelConfig

Navigate to the Backoffice application and switch to the Integration UI Tool. First, check if these integration objects are OAuth enabled by selecting "Integration APIs | Authentication":

If they are enabled for basic authentication, change the Authentication Type to "OAuth2" via ImpEx or manually:

Ensure all three Inbound Channel Configurations are updated:

Next, validate the exposed attributes of the integration objects. Navigate to "Integration Object Modeling | Type Modeling" and select, for example, the IntegrationService to view the exposed attributes.

Compare them with the required attributes (for later communication with the SAP CX AI Toolkit):

  • IntegrationService: authenticationType(code), integrationObject(classes, code, integrationType, items).
  • ScriptService: autodisabling, code, content, description, disabled, scriptType (code).
  • OutboundChannelConfig: autoGenerate, code, destination (additionalProperties, credentialConsumedOAuth, destinationTarget(id) , endpoint, id, url), integrationObject(id), synchronizeDelete)

E.g., the IntegrationService object should expose 2 attributes: authenticationType and the integrationObject, while the attribute authenticateType should further expose the code attribute and the attribute integrationObject should further expose the classes, code, integrationType and items attributes.

If they don't match, identify and expose the missing attributes by checking the boxes next to the required attributes in each of the three integration objects listed above.

Lastly, ensure the 'oAuthUrl' attribute is exposed as part of the ConsumedDestination attribute in the OutboundChannelConfig object using the provided ImpEx scripts below:

 

INSERT_UPDATE IntegrationObjectItemAttribute;integrationObjectItem(integrationObject(code), code)[unique = true];attributeName[unique = true];attributeDescriptor(enclosingType(code),qualifier)
;OutboundChannelConfig:ConsumedOAuthCredential;oAuthUrl;ConsumedOAuthCredential:oAuthUrl;

 

You can use the administration console (hac) to import the ImpEx scirpts.

1.3 Create the OAuthClient for authentication with CX AI Toolkit

To enable CX AI Toolkit to get the authentication and authorization key for data communication with SAP Commerce Cloud, we also need to create an OAuthClient.

First, I'll create an Employee user in the administration cockpit in Backoffice, this user will be used later to relate to the OAuthClient for access management.

I'll type in the necessary information including id, name, the default language (e.g., English) and currency (US dollar):

and ensure the user is in the admin group for demo purposes:

Next, I'll create a new OAuthClient by selecting "System | OAuth | OAuth Clients" and using the subtype "Integration Client Credentials Details":

Enter a meaningful OAuth client ID and secret (keep these for later setup on the CX AI toolkit side). Relate the newly created user to this OAuth client:

Provide the authority of ROLE_INTEGRATIONADMINGROUP and validate that the grant type is set as "client_credentials":

Enter the "basic" scope:

Set the access and refresh token validity, then finalize the creation of the OAuth client:

With these steps, the SAP Commerce Cloud server is ready for integration.

Step 2: Set Up the SAP CX AI Toolkit

I'll log into the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant with the received admin user credentials. If you are not sure how to get the access credentials, check the getting access guide. On the home page, I'll click the user account icon on the top right, then select Settings:

In the Settings workspace, I'll click Integrations and "Add Integration":

Enter a meaningful name and choose "SAP Commerce Cloud" as the integration type:

Now fill in the additional fields:

  • Host URL: The base URL where the 'odata2webservices' service is hosted (Check the 'manifest.json' file as discussed earlier. In my case, the service is hosted on the Backoffice node, so I can copy the domain URL of the Backoffice endpoint from the environment workspace on the Cloud Portal page.).
  • Client ID: The OAuth client ID created earlier.
  • Client Secret: The OAuth client secret.

Click "Test Connection" (this might take a few seconds until the successful message is displayed)

Once the connection is successful, choose the product catalogs and classification systems as the source:

Please remember to choose all the product catalogs and classification systems that you plan to use, as you won't be able to add more later. If additional product catalogs or classification systems are needed later, you'll have to delete the current integration and create a new one. In my case, I select the two sample B2C product catalogs and the default classification system.

Please remember to save the integration. Once saved, I can check the Integration list to verify that it is active.

That's how to set up the integration in the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant. Let's move on to the next step to see how to validate the connection and synchronize the data.

Step 3: Validate the Connection and Synchronize the Data

To confirm that the necessary integration components are created in SAP Commerce Cloud, log into Backoffice and switch to the Integration UI Tool, and navigate to "Outbound Sync | Configuration | Channels":

I can observe that two outbound channels for the two selected B2C product catalogs and one outbound channel for the default classification system have been created by the SAP CX AI Toolkit. To further verify this, I can check the Consumed Destination IDs (as shown above); I can notice that they correspond to the organization ID of the SAP CX AI Toolkit. This can be found under "Settings | Organization | Organization Details." in the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant:

Back to SAP Commerce Cloud Backoffice, under "Containers," "Streams," and "Jobs", I can also verify that the corresponding objects are created for data communication.

Lastly, let's visit "Outbound Sync | Scheduling | CronJobs" to see the created CronJobs responsible for data synchronization from SAP Commerce Cloud to SAP CX AI Toolkit:

These CronJobs are responsible for synchronizing product data with the SAP CX AI Toolkit. Depending on the number of product catalogs selected, an equivalent number of CronJobs will be created, with each CronJob corresponding to a specific product catalog. Additionally, I can observe two extra CronJobs: one related to the classification system attributes and another covering the entire classification system, including its structure, attributes and values.

By default, the CronJobs related to the classification system and its attributes start immediately after the integration is established. When these CronJobs are executed, they will also trigger the other product catalog-related CronJobs accordingly.

To validate whether the data has been partially synchronized to the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant, go to the tenant homepage and navigate to the Settings workspace, select "Commerce | Classification Attributes." These attributes come through the first CronJob, which should complete successfully within a few minutes. Click on the drop-down box of classification classes to view all synchronized classes.

The initial synchronization process, which continues until all the CronJobs are successfully executed, may take several hours depending on the volume of data. Although it's not recommended, it is possible to trigger the product catalog CronJobs manually or on demand. To do this, simply click on the 'Run CronJob' action button for the individual CronJob.

Conclusion

I have demonstrated the key steps needed to successfully integrate SAP Commerce Cloud with the SAP CX AI Toolkit. This setup will enable seamless AI-driven enhancements to your SAP Commerce Cloud platform.

To explore further how to use the SAP CX AI Toolkit in conjunction with SAP Commerce Cloud, please refer to the User Guide on the SAP CX AI Toolkit help portal. Additionally, keep an eye out for our upcoming learning journey about the SAP CX AI Toolkit on learning.sap.com.

If you found this blog post helpful, give it a thumbs-up. Thank you for reading!
